findings.description: New TCP connection + SMTP handshake created per message inside the flush loop (the SMTP equivalent of N+1): tens of ms overhead each, so a 10k-message flush wastes minutes on handshakes and churns 10k TIME_WAIT sockets risking ephemeral-port exhaustion — hoist a single `with smtplib.SMTP(SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT) as server:` outside the loop and reuse it for the whole batch (O(1) connections instead of O(n)).

`smtplib.SMTP(...)` is constructed with no `timeout=`, which defaults to block-forever, so a single unresponsive SMTP server hangs `flush()` (and the pipeline stage calling it) indefinitely — pass an explicit timeout (e.g. `timeout=10`).

The `_seen` dedup set is an unbounded cache (no max size, no TTL, never cleared by `flush()`), so a long-running pipeline accumulates every recipient ever enqueued (~100B+ per entry, ~1GB per 10M addresses) until `clear_dedup_cache()` is called manually — bound it (LRU/TTL) or clear it as part of flush if cross-flush dedup is not intended.

Sends are strictly serial (one blocking server round-trip per message even after connection reuse), capping throughput at roughly 10-50 msg/s; for bulk pipeline volumes consider a small pool of connections/workers or chunked sends.

`_pending` and `_seen` have no synchronization, so concurrent enqueue-during-flush from multiple pipeline threads races (lost/duplicated messages, set/list mutation during iteration) — low confidence since usage context is unknown; document single-threaded use or add a lock.

If any `sendmail` raises, the exception escapes `flush()` before `self._pending.clear()` runs, leaving already-sent messages queued so the next flush re-sends them as duplicates and unsent ones are stuck behind the failure — noted briefly, detail deferred to critic-logic.
Dedup key is `msg.to` alone, so a second, different message to the same recipient is silently dropped (and stays dropped across flushes because `_seen` is never reset) — deferred to critic-logic.

`msg.to` and `msg.subject` are interpolated raw into RFC-822 headers in `_render`, enabling CRLF header injection (the unused `MIMEText` import is what should have been used) — deferred to critic-security.
Plain SMTP on submission port 587 with no STARTTLS or authentication — deferred to critic-security.
Dead weight: `field` (line 5), `MIMEText` (line 6), the module-level `logger` (line 9), and the `Message.template` field (line 20) are declared and never used.
